title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT Moment" is coming for robotics but that humanoid robot development may be myopic. She contends that the real challenge is not intelligence but designing robots for delight and human interaction rather than utility alone, and that billions of robots will soon enter society requiring thoughtful design philosophy.
writeup: Stanford researcher Catie Cuan argues that the anticipated "ChatGPT moment" for robotics will not arrive through humanoid form factors alone, pushing back on the approach implied by companies like 1X and Unitree. Her central claim is that the binding constraint is not intelligence but design for human interaction: as billions of robots enter society, building for delight rather than utility alone becomes critical. The takeaway reframes the robotics challenge around how machines relate to people instead of treating capability or anthropomorphic shape as sufficient.
